data.title: Color Star Technology regains compliance with Nasdaq minimum price rule
data.description:
• Color Star Technology (NASDAQ:ADD) announced on Thursday, that it has received a formal notification from Nasdaq that the company has regained compliance with Nasdaq listing rule which requires the company’s ordinary shares to maintain a minimum bid price of $1.00 per share.
  • The Nasdaq staff made this determination of compliance after the closing bid price of the company’s Class A Ordinary Shares has been at $1.00 per share or greater for the last 10 consecutive business days from November 15, 2024 to November 29, 2024.

data.title: Credit Acceptance boosts, extends revolving warehouse facility
data.description:
Credit Acceptance (NASDAQ:CACC) increased the amount of its Warehouse Facility V, one of its revolving warehouse facilities, to $250M from $200M. The auto financing company said the interest rate on borrowings under the facility were reduced to SOFR + 185 basis points from SOFR +245 bps.

It also extended the date on which the facility will stop revolving to Dec. 29, 2027, from Dec. 29, 2025. The maturity of the facility was extended to Dec. 27, 2029, from Dec. 27, 2027.

There were no other material changes to the facility. As of Dec. 5, 2024, Credit Acceptance (NASDAQ:CACC) didn't have a balance outstanding under the facility.

data.title: Beneficient to acquire Mercantile Bank International for ~$1.5M
data.description:
Beneficient (NASDAQ:BENF) agreed to acquire Mercantile Bank International, a Puerto Rico-based international financial entity ("IFE"), for ~$1.5M, payable in up to ~2.1M shares of the company's class A common stock and cash, the company said Thursday.

The acquisition would position Beneficient (NASDAQ:BENF) to offer alternative asset custody services that include, among other potential items, a companion line of business focused on issuing depositary receipts to assist holders of foreign investments gain access to the capital markets of additional international jurisdictions. The company believes these alternative asset custody services may yield higher fee assessments than more traditional custody offerings.

"Our objective is to deliver additional alternative asset custody services to customers with the potential to generate higher fee rates than are generally available for traditional custody services," Benficient said.

After the deal close, Beneficient (BENF) plans to expand its offering of custody services to large institutional investors and the growing needs of third-party alternative trading systems and foreign securities exchanges, compared with its traditional focus on individual investors and small-to-mid-sized institutions.

An IFE’s authorized activities may include custody, clearing, and payments, and related traditional and digital products and services and, as approved by the Office of the Commissioner of Financial Institutions of Puerto Rico, traditional banking services, such as deposits, lending, investments, and trusts.

data.title: Rubrik surges as cybersecurity company ups guidance after strong Q3 results
data.description:
Rubrik (NYSE:RBRK) shares surged nearly 16% in late trading on Thursday after the cybersecurity company upped its guidance for fiscal 2025 after reporting stronger-than-expected third-quarter results.

The company now expects to generate between $860M and $862M in revenue for the full year, up from a previous view of $830M to $838M. The new guidance is above the $834.9M analysts were expecting.

On an adjusted basis, Rubrik now expects to lose between $1.82 and $1.86 per share, better than the prior view of an annual loss between $2.06 and $2.12 per share.

Subscription annual recurring revenue for the full-year is expected to be between $1.057B and $1.061B, while free cash flow is expected to be between a negative $39M and $45M, better than the previous range of a negative $57M to $67M.

“We’re incredibly proud to have surpassed $1 billion in Subscription ARR, growing 38% year-over-year,” said Bipul Sinha, Rubrik’s Chief Executive Officer, Chairman, and Co-Founder, in a statement. “This is a significant milestone achieved in just over 10 years since the company was founded. Our strong growth at scale shows that we’re winning the cyber resilience market and we’re excited to continue to execute on this new vision to define the future of the cybersecurity industry.”

For the third-quarter ending October 31, Rubrik said it lost an adjusted $0.21 per share as revenue rose 43% year-over-year to come in at $236.2M. Subscription revenue accounted for $221.5M of that figure, as the company had 2,085 customers with $100,000 or more in subscription annual recurring revenue.

A consensus of analysts expects the company to lose an adjusted $0.40 per share on $217.6M in revenue.

Looking to the fourth-quarter, Rubrik expects to lose between $0.37 and $0.41 per share on an adjusted basis, with revenue between $231.5M and $233.5M. The midpoint of $232.5M is above the $225M analysts were expecting, who also anticipated an adjusted loss of $0.41 per share.

The company will host a conference call at 5 p.m. EST to discuss the results.

data.title: Asana Non-GAAP EPS of -$0.02 beats by $0.05, revenue of $183.9M beats by $3.24M
data.description:
• Asana press release (NYSE:ASAN): Q3 Non-GAAP EPS of -$0.02 beats by $0.05.
  • Revenue of $183.9M (+10.5% Y/Y) beats by $3.24M.

  • For fiscal 2025, Asana expects:

  • Revenues of $723.0 million to $724.0 million, representing year over year growth of 11%.

  • Non-GAAP operating loss of $46.0 million to $45.0 million, with 6% operating loss margin.

  • Non-GAAP net loss per share of $0.15 to $0.14, assuming basic and diluted weighted average shares outstanding of approximately 229 million.

  • For the fourth quarter of fiscal 2025, Asana expects:

  • Revenues of $187.5 million to $188.5 million, representing year over year growth of 10%.
  • Non-GAAP operating loss of $6.5 million to $5.5 million, with 3% operating loss margin.
  • Non-GAAP net loss per share of $0.02 to $0.01, assuming basic and diluted weighted average shares outstanding of approximately 229 million.
